short_name: Degree data do not determine the audited structural profile
formal_statement: Among loopless labeled digraphs on five vertices, many fixed in/out-degree fibers contain graphs with different audited structural profiles.
closest_prior_literature: Graph realization and degree-sequence non-uniqueness
literature_review_status: PRIOR LITERATURE IDENTIFIED; CLAIM-SPECIFIC SEARCH PENDING
computational_support: 1,048,576 labeled digraphs; 225,025 degree fibers; 93,167 fibers with residual variation and 95,053 with full-profile variation.
assumptions_scope: n=5; loopless directed labeled graphs; profile definitions are implementation-specific.
allowed_claim: Degree information is insufficient for the particular structural observables audited at n=5.
forbidden_overclaim: A general asymptotic theorem, or a claim that degree data never identify any relevant structure.
editorial_action: KEEP AS SUPPORTING ENUMERATION; DEFINE EVERY PROFILE FORMALLY
next_action: Write explicit mathematical definitions of the residual and full structural profiles before citation.

short_name: Exact finite rate-distortion curves inside degree fibers
formal_statement: For selected n=5 degree fibers, exact optimization over all 52 partitions yields distortion curves as a function of entropy budget.
novelty_status: APPLICATION OF CLASSICAL THEORY; NO GENERAL NOVELTY CLAIM
closest_prior_literature: Shannon rate-distortion theory; finite partition optimization
computational_support: Exact curves for six control graphs and their degree fibers.
assumptions_scope: Chosen controls, n=5, chosen distortion and partition family.
allowed_claim: The stated finite optimization results are exact for the declared controls.
forbidden_overclaim: A new general rate-distortion theorem or a physical information law.
editorial_action: MOVE OUT OF MAIN FOUNDATIONAL NARRATIVE
next_action: Specify the distortion functional and explain why these controls matter, or retain only in the repository.

short_name: Finite projective-family polytope is a simplex
formal_statement: For a fixed maximum size N with induced-subgraph kernels, a finite projective family (p1,...,pN) is uniquely determined by arbitrary pN and is affinely isomorphic to the full simplex on unlabeled N-vertex classes.
novelty_status: LIKELY DIRECT COROLLARY OF PROJECTIVE KERNEL CONSTRUCTION
closest_prior_literature: Finite projective systems; Kolmogorov consistency; convex polytopes
literature_review_status: NOVELTY SEARCH REQUIRED BEFORE THEOREM CLAIM
computational_support: Composition checked exactly through N=4; polytope dimensions and extreme points enumerated.
assumptions_scope: Finite top size N; induced-subgraph kernels; no infinite extension claimed by this finite argument.
allowed_claim: Finite projectivity alone leaves the top-level distribution unconstrained and therefore does not select a unique law.
forbidden_overclaim: That projectivity never constrains infinite families, or that the result is a new extension theorem.
editorial_action: RECAST AS A SHORT PROPOSITION WITH PRIOR-WORK ATTRIBUTION
next_action: Compare explicitly with standard projective-limit constructions and exchangeable random graph theory.

short_name: Exchangeability and projectivity do not select a unique graph law
formal_statement: The independent directed Erdos-Renyi family indexed by p gives a continuum of distinct exchangeable, projective, nondegenerate laws.
closest_prior_literature: Exchangeable random graphs; Aldous-Hoover representation; Erdos-Renyi laws
computational_support: Finite symmetry-orbit dimensions enumerated through n=4; continuum counterfamily is analytic.
assumptions_scope: Directed loopless graph laws; selected reversal/complement symmetries are assumptions, not derived principles.
allowed_claim: The listed symmetries and finite projectivity are insufficient for unique law selection.
forbidden_overclaim: A claim that no additional principled axiom could select a law.
editorial_action: KEEP AS A SHORT CLASSICAL COUNTEREXAMPLE
next_action: Cite exchangeability literature and separate the analytic counterfamily from the finite orbit tables.

short_name: Condensation of a digraph defines a partial order
formal_statement: Strongly connected components form equivalence classes under mutual reachability, and the condensation graph is acyclic; quotient reachability is a partial order.
novelty_status: CLASSICAL; ATTRIBUTE, DO NOT CLAIM NOVELTY
closest_prior_literature: Strongly connected components and condensation DAGs
computational_support: Implementation self-tests and exhaustive n=5 enumeration support the code.
assumptions_scope: Finite directed graphs.
allowed_claim: A directed relation canonically induces an internal quotient order through SCC condensation.
forbidden_overclaim: Discovery of the SCC condensation theorem, physical time, or a unique temporal arrow.
editorial_action: STATE AS BACKGROUND PROPOSITION
next_action: Use a standard graph-theory citation and remove novelty language.

short_name: Full reachability preorder encodes SCC partition and quotient order
formal_statement: The reflexive reachability matrix on the original labeled vertices recovers SCC equivalence from its symmetric part and the condensation order from the quotient.
closest_prior_literature: Finite preorders and quotient posets
computational_support: Exhaustive code comparison for all n=5 digraphs.
assumptions_scope: Finite labeled digraphs.
allowed_claim: The full labeled reachability preorder is an injective representation of the complete labeled condensation structure.
forbidden_overclaim: That the representation is a newly discovered graph invariant.
editorial_action: KEEP AS DEFINITIONAL LEMMA

short_name: Count of full labeled condensation preorders
formal_statement: N_full(n) = sum_{k=1}^n S(n,k) p(k), where S(n,k) counts set partitions and p(k) counts partial orders on k labeled blocks.
novelty_status: LIKELY CLASSICAL ENUMERATION OF FINITE PREORDERS
closest_prior_literature: Enumeration of finite preorders and finite topologies
literature_review_status: DEDICATED NOVELTY SEARCH REQUIRED; EXPECT CLASSICAL
computational_support: Independent exhaustive enumeration gives N_full(5)=6,942.
assumptions_scope: Finite labeled sets; every preorder is realizable as reachability of a digraph.
allowed_claim: The formula and the n=5 count are correct for full labeled reachability preorders.
forbidden_overclaim: Presenting the formula as novel before checking finite-preorder literature.
editorial_action: KEEP, BUT ATTRIBUTE IF PRIOR FORMULA FOUND
next_action: Search the finite-preorder/topology enumeration literature and OEIS references.

short_name: Count of minimum-representative quotient-poset codes
formal_statement: N_rep(n) = sum_{k=1}^n binom(n-1,k-1) p(k) for the code that stores SCC minima and the quotient order but not full block membership.
novelty_status: POTENTIALLY ORIGINAL ELEMENTARY COROLLARY; SEARCH REQUIRED
closest_prior_literature: Finite posets with distinguished feasible minimum-label sets
computational_support: Independent exhaustive enumeration gives N_rep(5)=5,234.
assumptions_scope: Minimum vertex represents each SCC; labels are 0,...,n-1.
allowed_claim: The old 5,234 result exactly counts the declared intermediate code.
forbidden_overclaim: Calling 5,234 the number of full labeled condensation structures.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORRECTIVE PROPOSITION

short_name: Minimum-representative code is not injective
formal_statement: Different SCC partitions can have the same set of SCC minima and the same quotient order, hence the representative code can collapse distinct full labeled preorders.
closest_prior_literature: Information loss under quotient encodings
computational_support: Explicit pair plus complete fiber accounting: 5,234 codes represent 6,942 full structures.
assumptions_scope: The original representative encoding.
allowed_claim: The earlier wording was ambiguous because the implementation discarded SCC membership information.
forbidden_overclaim: Calling the discrepancy fabrication or treating all A8 outputs as invalid.
editorial_action: KEEP PROMINENTLY AS CORRECTION

short_name: Three distinct equivalence levels at n=5
formal_statement: For loopless labeled digraphs on five vertices: 6,942 full labeled reachability preorders, 5,234 minimum-representative codes, and 139 unlabeled preorder classes.
closest_prior_literature: Finite preorder enumeration
computational_support: All 2^20 loopless labeled digraphs enumerated; independent closed formulas reproduce two labeled counts.
assumptions_scope: n=5 only.
allowed_claim: These exact finite counts distinguish three well-defined quotient levels.
forbidden_overclaim: An asymptotic result or a physical prediction.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORE DATA TABLE

short_name: Single-edge flip changes the n=5 condensation preorder with probability 75/256
formal_statement: Under the uniform distribution over ordered pairs (G,e), where G is a loopless labeled n=5 digraph and e one of 20 directed non-loop edges, P[C(G) != C(G triangle e)] = 75/256.
closest_prior_literature: Random digraph perturbation and reliability of reachability
computational_support: 20,971,520 graph-edge pairs; 6,144,000 changes under both full and representative codes.
assumptions_scope: Uniform n=5 loopless labeled digraphs and uniform directed edge toggle.
allowed_claim: The fraction is exactly 75/256 for the declared finite ensemble.
forbidden_overclaim: A general formula, limiting probability, or universal robustness constant.
editorial_action: KEEP AS OPEN-PROBLEM MOTIVATION
next_action: Define P_n formally and seek a combinatorial decomposition or asymptotic theorem.

short_name: Candidate clocks are monotone on comparable pairs
formal_statement: Longest-chain depth, down/up balance, and mean linear-extension position increase strictly along comparable elements in the audited condensation posets.
mathematical_status: FINITE-CASE VERIFIED; GENERAL PARTS MOSTLY STANDARD
closest_prior_literature: Poset rank functions, linear extensions, order-preserving maps
literature_review_status: CLASSICAL ATTRIBUTION AND GENERAL PROOF REVIEW REQUIRED
computational_support: All 5,234 representative codes audited.
assumptions_scope: Finite posets; chosen normalizations.
allowed_claim: These functions provide legitimate ordinal coordinates on comparable pairs.
forbidden_overclaim: A unique clock, calibrated time, physical duration, or preferred temporal orientation.
editorial_action: COMPRESS TO DEFINITIONS AND ONE NONUNIQUENESS RESULT

short_name: Internal order does not select a unique scalar clock
formal_statement: The audited monotone clock candidates disagree on incomparable elements and differ in injectivity; 10.376457% of nontrivial n=5 codes give identical normalized values for all three.
closest_prior_literature: Order-preserving utility representations and poset dimension/rank
literature_review_status: NOVELTY SEARCH REQUIRED ONLY FOR THE FINITE STATISTICS
computational_support: 5,233 nontrivial representative codes; exact disagreement statistics.
assumptions_scope: n=5 and the three specified clock definitions.
allowed_claim: The condensation order alone does not choose among these natural scalarizations.
forbidden_overclaim: No scalar clock can ever be defined, or physical time is impossible.
editorial_action: KEEP AS FINITE COUNTEREVIDENCE, NOT UNIVERSAL NO-GO

short_name: Longest-chain duration is highly but not universally additive
formal_statement: For comparable triples in the n=5 audit, longest-chain duration is additive in 98.9281886% of cases; interval-volume duration is additive in 81.4576635%.
closest_prior_literature: Poset height, interval cardinality, ranked posets
computational_support: All nontrivial n=5 representative codes and comparable triples.
assumptions_scope: n=5; declared duration candidates.
allowed_claim: Longest-chain depth is the strongest audited ordinal duration candidate at this finite size.
forbidden_overclaim: Proper time, a physical metric, exact additivity in general, or continuum scaling.
editorial_action: KEEP SELECTED NUMBERS IN COMBINATORICS PAPER

short_name: No single multiplicative scale preserves all chain durations in the stated quotient example
formal_statement: For a<b<c<d coarse-grained by {a,b}|{c}|{d}, fine-to-quotient duration ratios differ (1/2 and 2/3), so one multiplicative scale cannot preserve both.
closest_prior_literature: Coarse-graining of path length and quotient metrics
computational_support: Exact four-element witness.
assumptions_scope: Longest-chain duration and the specified partition.
allowed_claim: Naive quotienting does not preserve all ordinal durations by a single scale factor.
forbidden_overclaim: A universal impossibility for every possible duration coarse-graining.
editorial_action: KEEP AS ILLUSTRATIVE COUNTEREXAMPLE

short_name: Broad interval-feature classifier did not satisfy all confirmatory gates
formal_statement: A broad learned classifier separated some synthetic ensembles but failed dimension-specific and finite-sample gates.
closest_prior_literature: Causal-set dimension estimators and interval abundance statistics
computational_support: Seeded train/test protocol with failed G4 and G6.
assumptions_scope: Declared synthetic generators, finite sizes, learned classifier.
allowed_claim: The broad protocol was insufficient under its frozen gates.
forbidden_overclaim: Evidence for or against manifold emergence in nature.
editorial_action: REMOVE FROM MAIN TEXT; RETAIN AS PROVENANCE

short_name: Selected interval features discriminate 2D sprinklings from selected nulls
formal_statement: Under the frozen A12 generators and matching rules, the targeted classifier passed all eight gates at n=96 and n=128.
closest_prior_literature: Causal-set interval abundance and dimension diagnostics
computational_support: 8/8 gates; seeded paired synthetic evaluations.
assumptions_scope: Only the selected 2D and null ensembles at the tested sizes.
allowed_claim: The feature family has targeted discriminatory power for these models.
forbidden_overclaim: A necessary or sufficient test for manifold-likeness.
editorial_action: SUMMARIZE BRIEFLY OR OMIT FROM FOUNDATIONAL PAPER

short_name: A scalar analytic interval signature failed selected null rejection
formal_statement: The A13 analytic discrepancy accepted 2D holdouts but did not reject transitive percolation at the required rate and failed an adversarial matching gate.
novelty_status: NO NOVELTY CLAIM BEYOND DOCUMENTED FAILURE
closest_prior_literature: Causal-set interval abundance formulas
computational_support: 7/9 gates; exact normalization and Monte Carlo validation passed.
assumptions_scope: Declared reference and null generators.
allowed_claim: The tested scalar signature is insufficient.
forbidden_overclaim: A general impossibility theorem for analytic signatures.
editorial_action: MOVE TO FAILURE HISTORY

short_name: A covariance-aware multivariate signature passed its targeted model checks
formal_statement: A Mahalanobis-type interval signature calibrated only on reference samples passed all nine A14 gates against selected nulls.
canonical_version: A14 canonical but insufficient after A15
closest_prior_literature: Multivariate goodness-of-fit and causal-set interval statistics
computational_support: 9/9 frozen gates across declared sizes and nulls.
assumptions_scope: Finite synthetic ensembles; selected bins and covariance estimator.
allowed_claim: The signature is a useful necessary model check against those nulls.
forbidden_overclaim: A sufficient certificate of 2D embeddability or manifold-likeness.
editorial_action: PRESENT ONLY TO MOTIVATE THE COUNTEREXAMPLE

short_name: Global interval statistics can accept orders with local non-2D obstructions
formal_statement: A15 inserts certified S3-type obstruction gadgets into transitive orders and finds prospective false positives for the A14 global signature.
novelty_status: PROJECT-SPECIFIC COUNTEREXAMPLE; CONSTRUCTION MAY BE ORIGINAL
closest_prior_literature: Order dimension; forbidden subposets; causal-set manifold-likeness tests
computational_support: Prospective confirmation and obstruction certificates; 7/8 gates, with density matching intentionally not always met.
assumptions_scope: Specific gadget construction and global signature.
allowed_claim: Passing the global interval statistic is not sufficient for local 2D order structure.
forbidden_overclaim: A complete forbidden-suborder characterization of manifold-likeness.
editorial_action: KEEP AS THE MAIN LESSON OF A12-A17

short_name: Local and global checks still admitted clustered exact 2D counterexamples
formal_statement: The A16 complementarity protocol detected several planted defects but failed to reject the bimodal exact-2D family under its target gate.
closest_prior_literature: Local versus global goodness-of-fit diagnostics
computational_support: 9/10 gates; targeted bimodal rejection failed.
assumptions_scope: Specified local channels and synthetic families.
allowed_claim: Adding local checks did not solve the selected global-signature ambiguity.
forbidden_overclaim: That local diagnostics can never complement global statistics.
editorial_action: REMOVE FROM MAIN TEXT; RETAIN AS DEVELOPMENT HISTORY

short_name: Multiscale self-similarity detects some heterogeneous regimes but not compact clustering
formal_statement: A17 rejected the opposing-diagonal regime but accepted most bimodal and four-corner exact 2D challenges.
closest_prior_literature: Multiscale goodness-of-fit and self-similarity tests
computational_support: 6/9 gates; challenge families were exact 2D orders.
assumptions_scope: Declared scales, calibration, and synthetic families.
allowed_claim: The tested multiscale statistic is sensitive to some regime heterogeneity but not to all compact mixtures.
forbidden_overclaim: A universal multiscale no-go.
editorial_action: KEEP AS NEGATIVE METHOD RESULT, NOT CORE THEORY

short_name: Strict coordinatewise monotone transforms preserve product order
formal_statement: For latent coordinates x,y, replacing each coordinate by a strictly increasing function leaves every pairwise product-order comparison unchanged.
novelty_status: CLASSICAL; ATTRIBUTE, DO NOT CLAIM NUMERICAL VALIDATION AS EVIDENCE
closest_prior_literature: Order isomorphisms; probability integral transform
computational_support: 270 paired transforms verified implementation invariance.
assumptions_scope: Strictly increasing coordinate transforms.
allowed_claim: Order-only data are invariant under monotone recalibration of latent marginals.
forbidden_overclaim: A new theorem established by 270 simulations.
editorial_action: REPLACE AUDIT EMPHASIS WITH A SHORT PROOF

short_name: The induced order law depends on the copula, not continuous marginals
formal_statement: For fixed copula C, continuous strictly increasing marginal changes leave the ensemble law of the induced coordinatewise order unchanged.
closest_prior_literature: Sklar's theorem and copula invariance
computational_support: Seeded ensemble checks support implementation only.
assumptions_scope: Continuous marginals and fixed copula.
allowed_claim: Order observations cannot identify latent marginal scales within a fixed copula class.
forbidden_overclaim: A new copula theorem or recovery of spacetime measure from order alone.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CLASSICAL PILLAR WITH CLEAR ATTRIBUTION

short_name: No deterministic order-only estimator can distinguish monotone-equivalent latent models
formal_statement: If two latent models induce the same finite relation matrix, every deterministic function of that matrix and its relabel-invariant observables gives the same output.
closest_prior_literature: Statistical identifiability and invariance
computational_support: Exact paired constructions and implementation checks.
assumptions_scope: Estimator receives only the relation/order object.
allowed_claim: Additional non-order information is necessary to identify latent marginal quantities.
forbidden_overclaim: That every physically relevant measure is forever unrecoverable from any enriched relational model.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CENTRAL, BUT LABEL AS IDENTIFIABILITY COROLLARY

short_name: Equivariant endogenous dynamics preserve observational equivalence
formal_statement: If two initial states are related by a symmetry and the complete update law and noise coupling are equivariant, their trajectories remain symmetry-related and invariant observables remain equal.
novelty_status: LIKELY STANDARD GROUP-EQUIVARIANCE PRINCIPLE; PROJECT APPLICATION
closest_prior_literature: Equivariant dynamical systems and coupling arguments
literature_review_status: DEDICATED NOVELTY SEARCH REQUIRED; EXPECT CLASSICAL
computational_support: Seeded trajectory pairs and gate tests.
assumptions_scope: Full state, update, initialization, and noise law respect the same transformation.
allowed_claim: Purely endogenous equivariant evolution cannot break the targeted order-preserving symmetry.
forbidden_overclaim: That dynamics can never break symmetry when stochastic laws, boundary conditions, or new primitives are non-equivariant.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CONDITIONAL THEOREM WITH GROUP-THEORY ATTRIBUTION

short_name: A calibrated non-invariant mark can break order degeneracy
formal_statement: A binary mark defined by a fixed external threshold distinguishes monotone-equivalent latent representations when the threshold is not transformed covariantly.
closest_prior_literature: Ancillary statistics and symmetry-breaking reference structures
computational_support: Synthetic witnesses and admissibility gates.
assumptions_scope: The mark is genuinely calibrated and independent of the order-only data.
allowed_claim: Additional calibrated structure can break the degeneracy.
forbidden_overclaim: That the required calibration has been physically derived.
editorial_action: KEEP TO CLARIFY WHAT INFORMATION IS MISSING

short_name: An independent q field can evade the order-only identifiability limit
formal_statement: If q contains cardinal contrasts not measurable from the order, q differences can distinguish states that the order alone identifies.
closest_prior_literature: Marked relational structures and gauge fields
literature_review_status: NOVELTY SEARCH REQUIRED FOR FORMULATION, NOT BASIC LOGIC
computational_support: Constructed paired states and gauge tests.
assumptions_scope: q is an additional primitive, not secretly computed from the order.
allowed_claim: q can supply relative quantitative information absent from order.
forbidden_overclaim: That the physical origin or empirical meaning of q has been derived.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORE DEFINITIONAL STEP

short_name: Global q offset is unobservable under contrast-only constructions
formal_statement: q_i -> q_i + c leaves centered contrasts and suitably normalized q-shape observables unchanged.
closest_prior_literature: Gauge redundancy and affine reparameterization
computational_support: Exact numerical gauge tests.
assumptions_scope: Only contrast- or normalized-q observables are admitted.
allowed_claim: The current construction determines q only up to a global offset.
forbidden_overclaim: A deep independent no-go, or proof that nature has this gauge.
editorial_action: STATE AS CONTRACT, NOT DISCOVERY

short_name: D_eff scales covariantly and normalized D_hat is gauge invariant
formal_statement: For edge costs ell_ij = exp(q_ij), q -> q+c gives D_eff -> exp(c) D_eff, while a homogeneous normalization removes the global factor.
source_audits: A23 and legacy RZS scale-gauge track
novelty_status: ELEMENTARY CONSEQUENCE OF EXPONENTIAL EDGE COSTS
closest_prior_literature: Shortest paths and conformal/global scale transformations
computational_support: Scale-gauge tests in the RZS track and A23.
assumptions_scope: Positive exponential edge costs and homogeneous normalization.
allowed_claim: D_eff is a computational round-trip relational cost defined up to global scale.
forbidden_overclaim: Physical distance, a Lorentzian metric, calibrated length, or spacetime geometry.
editorial_action: KEEP WITH STRONG TERMINOLOGY CONTROL

short_name: Exponential transition log-odds encode q contrasts
formal_statement: For P(i->j) proportional to A_ij exp(-beta q_ij), log[P(i->j)/P(i->k)] = log(A_ij/A_ik) - beta(q_ij-q_ik) when both alternatives are allowed.
novelty_status: CLASSICAL MULTINOMIAL-LOGIT IDENTITY APPLIED TO q
closest_prior_literature: Multinomial logit, Boltzmann/softmax kernels
computational_support: Corrected recovery and operational gates.
assumptions_scope: Kernel form and calibrated beta are supplied.
allowed_claim: The exponential kernel makes q contrasts operational in transition odds.
forbidden_overclaim: That the kernel or beta follows from the RZS primitives.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CANDIDATE MODEL WITH CLASSICAL ATTRIBUTION

short_name: Raw q scale and exponential strength are jointly degenerate
formal_statement: The product beta(q_j-q_k) is invariant under q -> s q and beta -> beta/s, so raw-score data do not separately identify q scale and beta.
closest_prior_literature: Scale non-identifiability in generalized linear choice models
literature_review_status: CLASSICAL ATTRIBUTION / STATISTICAL IDENTIFIABILITY SEARCH REQUIRED
computational_support: Synthetic parameter-recovery experiments.
assumptions_scope: Unstandardized q and exponential kernel.
allowed_claim: Only the product of raw-score scale and coupling strength is identifiable without calibration.
forbidden_overclaim: That lambda is always pure gauge after any normalization.
editorial_action: KEEP AS SHORT IDENTIFIABILITY LEMMA

short_name: Scale-free standardized q scores admit infinitely many local kernels
formal_statement: If z is a locally standardized q score, any positive admissible function f can define P_j proportional to f(z_j), so locality and affine invariance do not select a unique transition law.
closest_prior_literature: Choice models and invariant score transformations
computational_support: Thirty constructed observable/kernel combinations.
assumptions_scope: Specified local standardization, relabel equivariance, and positivity.
allowed_claim: The audited symmetry and locality principles leave the kernel law underdetermined.
forbidden_overclaim: That no additional axiom can select a law.
editorial_action: KEEP AS SETUP FOR LUCE/IIA RESULT

short_name: Difference-based odds and IIA select an exponential score family
formal_statement: If pairwise odds are context-independent and depend continuously on score differences through a multiplicative composition law, the positive solution is f(z)=C exp(-lambda z), with lambda free.
closest_prior_literature: Luce choice axiom, IIA, multinomial logit, exponential Cauchy equation
computational_support: Functional self-tests and alternative-principle audits.
assumptions_scope: Continuity/regularity, positivity, difference dependence, multiplicative odds composition.
allowed_claim: These axioms narrow the family to exponentials but do not fix lambda.
forbidden_overclaim: A wholly new derivation of multinomial logit or proof that nature obeys IIA.
editorial_action: REWRITE AS CLASSICAL THEOREM WITH PROJECT-SPECIFIC CONSEQUENCE

short_name: After local standardization, lambda is an identifiable dimensionless model parameter but remains underived
formal_statement: Standardizing q removes raw affine scale freedom; distinct lambda values then produce observationally distinct kernels and can be estimated from synthetic data.
closest_prior_literature: Identifiability in discrete-choice models
computational_support: Synthetic recovery RMSE and model comparisons.
assumptions_scope: Correct kernel family, sufficient data, chosen standardization, no misspecification.
allowed_claim: lambda is not removable after standardization and must be estimated or derived separately.
forbidden_overclaim: A fundamental constant or a value predicted by the current theory.
editorial_action: KEEP AS STATUS RESULT; MOVE RMSE DETAILS TO SUPPLEMENT

short_name: No nontrivial continuous cardinal score satisfies both extension stability and full positive-affine invariance
formal_statement: Under the A28 definitions, invariance under all q -> a q+b with a>0 and stability under arbitrary context extension force a continuous cardinal score to be trivial.
novelty_status: POTENTIALLY ORIGINAL FORMULATION; HIGH-PRIORITY NOVELTY SEARCH
closest_prior_literature: Measurement invariance, functional equations, context effects in choice theory
computational_support: Exact witness tests and frozen gates support the proof implementation.
assumptions_scope: The precise continuity, invariance, and extension-stability axioms stated in A28.
allowed_claim: The three audited requirements are jointly incompatible for a nontrivial cardinal score.
forbidden_overclaim: No meaningful score can ever be defined, or the theorem applies outside its explicit axioms.
editorial_action: PROMOTE TO A CENTRAL THEOREM AFTER LITERATURE REVIEW
next_action: Conduct a focused search in measurement theory, quasi-arithmetic means, and context-independent choice.

short_name: Additive base measure restores exact clone/refinement invariance for weighted messages
formal_statement: Replacing listing counts by additive masses mu and aggregating weighted sufficient statistics makes exact splitting of a state into same-q children representation-invariant.
closest_prior_literature: Measure-preserving refinement and sufficient statistics
computational_support: Exact refinement tests; overall protocol remains FAIL because of nondegeneracy boundary.
assumptions_scope: Positive additive masses and well-defined weighted context statistics.
allowed_claim: Base-measure weighting removes naive clone-count dependence.
forbidden_overclaim: That the origin or physical semantics of mu are derived.
editorial_action: KEEP POSITIVE IDENTITY; REPORT OVERALL FAILURE HONESTLY

short_name: Weighted standardization is singular at zero variance and not uniformly stable near vanishing mass
formal_statement: When weighted q variance is zero, z-scores are undefined; arbitrarily small-mass refinements can create unstable standardized coordinates near that boundary.
closest_prior_literature: Instability of normalized statistics near degenerate variance
computational_support: Boundary and near-boundary numerical witnesses.
assumptions_scope: Weighted z-score construction.
allowed_claim: The proposed standardized message needs an explicit nondegeneracy domain or a different boundary treatment.
forbidden_overclaim: That all weighted coarse-graining is unstable.
editorial_action: KEEP AS TECHNICAL LIMIT OR APPENDIX RESULT

short_name: Relation plus q cannot distinguish descriptive clones from ontically distinct copies
formal_statement: Two refined structures can be isomorphic as relation-plus-q marked objects while differing only in the intended interpretation of repeated states; invariant observables cannot recover that semantic distinction.
novelty_status: POTENTIALLY ORIGINAL FRAMEWORK FORMULATION; BASIC LOGIC IS GENERAL
closest_prior_literature: Indiscernibility, quotient representations, multiplicity measures
computational_support: Clone constructions and failed order/q-derived candidate measures.
assumptions_scope: No external multiplicity labels, transport history, or base mass supplied.
allowed_claim: An additional refinement/multiplicity semantics is required to distinguish the two interpretations.
forbidden_overclaim: Proof that copies can never be physically distinguished by any enriched future theory.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORE MOTIVATION FOR mu

short_name: Positive leaf weights have a unique additive extension on a finite refinement tree
formal_statement: Given positive terminal weights w_l, mu(A)=sum_{l descendant of A} w_l is the unique finitely additive extension consistent with those leaf weights and is independent of refinement order.
novelty_status: CLASSICAL FINITE ADDITIVITY RESULT; PROJECT APPLICATION
closest_prior_literature: Measures on finite trees and hierarchical aggregation
literature_review_status: CLASSICAL ATTRIBUTION / NOVELTY CLAIM NOT NEEDED
computational_support: 2,500 trees, multiple merge orders, commutation and dynamic splitting tests.
assumptions_scope: Finite rooted refinement tree and supplied terminal weights.
allowed_claim: Once leaf weights are supplied, additive mass is path-independent and uniquely determined.
forbidden_overclaim: That terminal leaves or their weights are physically selected.
editorial_action: RECAST AS LEMMA, MOVE LARGE TEST TABLES TO REPOSITORY

short_name: Consistent finite-branching refinements define a projective measure without terminal leaves
formal_statement: A consistent family of cylinder probabilities on an infinite finitely branching refinement tree extends to a measure on infinite paths.
closest_prior_literature: Kolmogorov extension theorem and probability on trees
computational_support: Constructed atomic and non-atomic examples; numerical martingale checks.
assumptions_scope: Projective consistency and regular measurable state spaces.
allowed_claim: Terminal ontology is not mathematically required for a projective measure.
forbidden_overclaim: A new extension theorem or proof that physical reality is infinitely divisible.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CLASSICAL PILLAR, NOT NEW THEOREM

short_name: Atomicity and terminality are logically distinct
formal_statement: An infinite nonterminal branch can retain positive atom mass; conversely a terminal-free refinement with uniformly shrinking cylinder masses can be non-atomic.
novelty_status: CLEAN FRAMEWORK-SPECIFIC ILLUSTRATION OF CLASSICAL DISTINCTION
closest_prior_literature: Atoms in product measures and measures on trees
computational_support: Telescoping product example converging exactly to 1/2 and non-atomic bounded-branching example.
assumptions_scope: Declared branch probabilities.
allowed_claim: Terminal nodes and measure atoms must not be identified.
forbidden_overclaim: A classification of all refinement measures.
editorial_action: KEEP AS A STRONG EXAMPLE

short_name: Exact regrouping consistency gives conditional ratios of additive weights
formal_statement: If positive subtree weights add under partitions, the consistent branch fraction is p(B|A)=W(B)/W(A).
closest_prior_literature: Conditional probability from finite additive measures
computational_support: 2,200 configurations and multiple regrouping trees; exact errors near machine precision.
assumptions_scope: Positive additive subtree weights.
allowed_claim: Projective regrouping selects the ratio architecture once W is given.
forbidden_overclaim: That projectivity selects the terminal weights or their physical origin.
editorial_action: STATE AS CONDITIONAL-MEASURE LEMMA

short_name: W_lambda(B)=sum mu_l exp(-lambda q_l) is exactly projective and gauge covariant
formal_statement: The partition sum is additive over disjoint descendants, obeys exact recursive aggregation, and under q->q+c scales by exp(-lambda c), leaving normalized branch fractions invariant.
novelty_status: CLASSICAL LOG-SUM-EXP / PARTITION-FUNCTION ALGEBRA APPLIED TO FRAMEWORK
closest_prior_literature: Partition functions and additive measures
computational_support: Maximum regrouping TV about 3.3e-16; gauge and message recursion tests.
assumptions_scope: Supplied mu, q, and lambda.
allowed_claim: This weight is an exact static projective message for the chosen exponential law.
forbidden_overclaim: Thermodynamic energy, physical temperature, or a derived fundamental law.
editorial_action: KEEP AS BRIDGE TO Q_lambda

short_name: Projectivity does not determine terminal weights or their ontology
formal_statement: Distinct positive terminal-weight assignments satisfy the same ratio and regrouping laws but induce different branch probabilities.
closest_prior_literature: Underdetermination of measures by consistency axioms
computational_support: Alternative weight systems show median total-variation separation around 0.399 in the A33 ensemble.
assumptions_scope: Projective architecture fixed; terminal weights free.
allowed_claim: Consistency constrains how weights combine, not which weights nature uses.
forbidden_overclaim: That no physical or statistical principle could determine weights.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CENTRAL LIMIT

short_name: Q_lambda is statically associative
formal_statement: Q_lambda(B)=-(1/lambda) log[(sum_i mu_i exp(-lambda q_i))/(sum_i mu_i)] gives the same result under direct or hierarchical aggregation when each block passes total mass and Q_lambda.
closest_prior_literature: Kolmogorov-Nagumo means and log-sum-exp aggregation
computational_support: Associativity error <=7.1e-14 across the A34 ensemble.
assumptions_scope: Positive masses and fixed lambda.
allowed_claim: Q_lambda is an exact static sufficient score for the corresponding partition sum.
forbidden_overclaim: A unique coarse-graining variable for every observable or a thermodynamic state variable.
editorial_action: KEEP, BUT ATTRIBUTE QUASI-ARITHMETIC STRUCTURE

short_name: Q_lambda(aq+c)=c+a Q_{a lambda}(q)
formal_statement: For a>0 and any offset c, the effective score transforms by Q_lambda(aq+c)=c+a Q_{a lambda}(q).
novelty_status: POTENTIALLY ORIGINAL EMPHASIS; ALGEBRA ITSELF ELEMENTARY
closest_prior_literature: Log moment-generating functions and entropic risk measures
computational_support: Maximum numerical identity error 2.66e-15.
assumptions_scope: Fixed positive masses; finite partition sum.
allowed_claim: The scale transformation changes the effective lambda and therefore couples score scale to the parameter.
forbidden_overclaim: A physical renormalization-group law.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORE IDENTITY

short_name: Q'_lambda=(1-a) q_bar + a Q_{a lambda}
formal_statement: Under the centered affine contraction q'_i=(1-a) q_bar + a q_i, the effective score obeys Q'_lambda=(1-a) q_bar+a Q_{a lambda}(q).
novelty_status: POTENTIALLY ORIGINAL AND TECHNICALLY STRONG; NOVELTY SEARCH REQUIRED
closest_prior_literature: Log-Laplace transforms under affine maps; entropic risk measures
computational_support: Exact numerical verification to 5.6e-17.
assumptions_scope: Declared centered affine contraction; no general nonlinear dynamics.
allowed_claim: The contraction transports the whole Q(lambda) curve by rescaling lambda.
forbidden_overclaim: A fundamental physical evolution equation or closure of the full RZS dynamics.
editorial_action: PROMOTE TO CENTRAL PROPOSITION AFTER LITERATURE REVIEW

short_name: A single Q_lambda is not generally a dynamically closed state variable
formal_statement: Distinct microdistributions can have the same mass, mean, and Q_lambda at one lambda but produce different future Q_lambda under the same contraction.
closest_prior_literature: Moment problems, sufficient statistics, closure of log-Laplace transforms
computational_support: Matched-state witnesses; minimum future difference 0.007059 and broader ensemble differences.
assumptions_scope: General non-Gaussian microdistributions; specified contraction.
allowed_claim: One point of the Q curve is insufficient for exact dynamics in general.
forbidden_overclaim: No finite-dimensional closure can exist under any restricted distribution family.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CENTRAL THEOREM/COUNTEREXAMPLE

short_name: Q_lambda depends on the cumulant hierarchy
formal_statement: Where the log moment-generating expansion exists, Q_lambda = mean - (lambda/2) variance + (lambda^2/6) kappa_3 - (lambda^3/24) kappa_4 + ... .
closest_prior_literature: Cumulant-generating functions
computational_support: Approximation errors decrease as kappa_3 and kappa_4 are included.
assumptions_scope: Exponential moments and convergence/validity of the expansion.
allowed_claim: Higher cumulants explain the generic failure of mean-variance closure.
forbidden_overclaim: A globally convergent expansion for heavy-tailed laws.
editorial_action: KEEP AS DERIVATION SUPPORT, NOT NEW RESULT

short_name: Gaussian affine dynamics close exactly in mean and variance
formal_statement: If q is Gaussian and innovations are Gaussian under an affine update, the state remains Gaussian and Q_lambda=mean-(lambda/2)variance is exactly determined by the first two moments.
closest_prior_literature: Gaussian AR processes and cumulant-generating functions
computational_support: Exact closure identity error <=1.78e-15.
assumptions_scope: Gaussian initial law, Gaussian affine innovations, finite variance.
allowed_claim: Mean-variance closure is exact inside the Gaussian invariant family.
forbidden_overclaim: That centering, finite variance, or the present RZS law derives Gaussianity.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CONDITIONAL SPECIAL CASE

short_name: Centering does not Gaussianize innovations or eliminate higher cumulants
formal_statement: Affine recursions with non-Gaussian innovations retain law-specific stationary cumulants; centered Rademacher components remain strongly non-Gaussian.
closest_prior_literature: AR(1) cumulants and linear processes
computational_support: Stationary Rademacher excess kurtosis -0.3800669; finite-dimensional centered components remain non-Gaussian.
assumptions_scope: Affine independent innovations; stated distributions.
allowed_claim: Centered noise is not equivalent to Gaussian noise.
forbidden_overclaim: A universal statement about every interacting RZS graph process.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORRECTION TO A COMMON ASSUMPTION

short_name: Gaussianity is selected only by additional strong assumption packages
formal_statement: Fixed-variance maximum entropy, exact finite-variance stability under normalized sums, or rotational invariance plus independent components can select Gaussian laws; each conclusion is conditional on its assumptions.
closest_prior_literature: Maximum entropy, stable laws, Maxwell characterization
computational_support: Entropy calculations, characteristic-function stability tests, corrected CLT diagnostic.
assumptions_scope: Each theorem's own assumptions; finite variance where stated.
allowed_claim: The framework can list sufficient Gaussian-selection principles and identify which are currently absent.
forbidden_overclaim: That the present RZS dynamics derives Gaussian noise.
editorial_action: KEEP AS SYNTHESIS; REDUCE AUDIT LOG IN MAIN TEXT

short_name: Central-limit convergence is asymptotic and requires independence or mixing plus tail conditions
formal_statement: Finite-variance normalized sums can approach Gaussian characteristic functions, but finite-n lattice effects remain and weak convergence alone does not control exponential moments.
closest_prior_literature: Lindeberg-Feller CLT and uniform integrability
computational_support: Compact characteristic-function error decreases through n=2048 for bounded/light-tailed laws.
assumptions_scope: Independent normalized sums; stated moment and tail controls.
allowed_claim: Gaussian universality is a conditional asymptotic regime, not an automatic consequence of centering.
forbidden_overclaim: Exact finite-scale Gaussianity or convergence of all nonlinear observables.
editorial_action: KEEP AS BACKGROUND FOR A37 RESULT

short_name: Matched mean and variance universalize low-order linear observables but not nonlinear/tail observables
formal_statement: In the affine stationary audit, variance and autocorrelation are law-independent by construction, while excess kurtosis, Q_lambda, tail behavior, and standardized kernel statistics remain law-sensitive.
novelty_status: PROJECT-SPECIFIC SYNTHESIS OF CLASSICAL LINEAR-PROCESS FACTS
closest_prior_literature: Linear processes, universality, cumulants
computational_support: Stationary excess spread 0.95017; Q spread at lambda=2 is 0.40743; kernel concentration spread about 0.02098.
assumptions_scope: Affine scalar/vector surrogate with a=0.825 and specified innovation laws.
allowed_claim: Noise details can be dispensable for some observables and essential for others.
forbidden_overclaim: Universality of the full RZS graph dynamics.
editorial_action: KEEP AS QUALIFIED OBSERVABLE-DEPENDENCE RESULT

short_name: Finite variance does not guarantee finite Q_lambda
formal_statement: Student-t innovations can have finite variance and finite fourth moment while E[exp(-lambda X)] diverges for every nonzero real lambda, making the population Q_lambda non-finite.
closest_prior_literature: Moment-generating functions of heavy-tailed distributions
computational_support: Analytic divergence classification; no finite-sample convergence claim substituted.
assumptions_scope: Student-t-type polynomial tails and real exponential moment.
allowed_claim: The partition-score formalism requires stronger tail control than finite variance.
forbidden_overclaim: That every heavy-tailed alternative is inadmissible for every observable.
editorial_action: KEEP AS STRONG DOMAIN-OF-DEFINITION WARNING

short_name: Shared non-Gaussian factors can block Gaussian coarse-graining
formal_statement: For Xi=sqrt(rho)C+sqrt(1-rho)Gi with common non-Gaussian C, variance-normalized block sums retain a non-Gaussian common component as block size grows.
closest_prior_literature: Dependent CLTs and factor models
computational_support: At block size 1024, standardized excess kurtosis approaches -2 in the audited rho=0.25 example.
assumptions_scope: Persistent common factor; no vanishing dependence.
allowed_claim: Independence or adequate mixing is a substantive requirement for Gaussian universality.
forbidden_overclaim: That all correlated systems fail to Gaussianize.
editorial_action: KEEP AS ONE CLEAN COUNTEREXAMPLE

short_name: Translation-covariant decomposable quasi-arithmetic means reduce to arithmetic or exponential families
formal_statement: Within the continuous strictly monotone weighted quasi-arithmetic class, decomposability plus M(q+c)=M(q)+c yields affine generators (arithmetic mean) or exponential generators (log-sum-exp family), up to equivalent reparameterization.
closest_prior_literature: Quasi-arithmetic means and translation invariance
computational_support: Associativity and gauge checks for arithmetic, exponential, median, minimum, and RMS.
assumptions_scope: Continuous strictly monotone quasi-arithmetic means; exact decomposability.
allowed_claim: The audited regular mean class contains two translation-covariant families relevant to the framework.
forbidden_overclaim: A new classification theorem or uniqueness of a physical coarse-graining.
editorial_action: REWRITE AS CLASSICAL THEOREM WITH CONSEQUENCES

short_name: Q_lambda exactly preserves the exponential kernel under grouping
formal_statement: If block mass mu_B and Q_B are passed, then mu_B exp(-lambda Q_B)=sum_{i in B} mu_i exp(-lambda q_i), so block probabilities equal direct microscopic aggregation.
closest_prior_literature: Sufficient statistics and log-sum-exp aggregation
computational_support: Maximum macro-probability TV about 2.19e-16.
assumptions_scope: Fixed exponential kernel and supplied mass.
allowed_claim: Q_lambda is the exact static message for this declared observable.
forbidden_overclaim: The unique coarse variable for every law or a physical thermodynamic potential.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORE POSITIVE RESULT

short_name: Mass plus weighted median is not a closed hierarchical message
formal_statement: There exist two groups whose group masses and lower weighted medians yield hierarchical median 0 while the direct leaf median is 1.
closest_prior_literature: Non-decomposability of quantiles
computational_support: Exact counterexample difference 1; random associativity pass rate 0.3822.
assumptions_scope: Lower weighted median summarized only by mass and median.
allowed_claim: Median is robust but that scalar summary is not hierarchically closed.
forbidden_overclaim: No hierarchical quantile sketch or richer summary can exist.
editorial_action: KEEP AS SHORT COUNTEREXAMPLE

short_name: A micro kernel and partition do not determine a unique macro transition without source occupancy
formal_statement: K_macro(A,B)=sum_{i in A} rho(i|A) sum_{j in B} K_ij depends explicitly on the conditional source distribution rho inside A.
closest_prior_literature: Markov-chain lumpability and coarse-graining
computational_support: Same micro kernel and partition with two occupancies gives median macro-row TV about 0.10122.
assumptions_scope: General non-lumpable finite Markov kernel.
allowed_claim: Occupancy or a lumpability condition is necessary for a unique macro Markov kernel.
forbidden_overclaim: A new discovery of lumpability or proof that pi is a fundamental physical field.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CLASSICAL PILLAR FOR pi

short_name: Joint flows aggregate exactly when occupancy is supplied
formal_statement: With F_ij=pi_i K_ij, define F_AB=sum_{i in A,j in B}F_ij and pi_A=sum_{i in A}pi_i; then K_macro(A,B)=F_AB/pi_A equals direct occupancy-weighted aggregation.
closest_prior_literature: Markov aggregation and probability flux
computational_support: Maximum aggregation error 8.88e-16.
assumptions_scope: pi_A>0 and a supplied micro occupancy distribution.
allowed_claim: Carrying joint flow or occupancy restores exact dynamic aggregation.
forbidden_overclaim: That the physical origin, dynamics, or uniqueness of pi is known.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CORE POSITIVE RESULT

short_name: There is no observable-independent unique scalar coarse-graining of q
formal_statement: Arithmetic mean exactly preserves the first moment, Q_lambda preserves the exponential kernel, minimum preserves an extremum, and different summaries answer different preservation problems.
mathematical_status: SUPPORTED BY EXACT IDENTITIES AND COUNTEREXAMPLES
closest_prior_literature: Sufficient statistics, coarse-graining, quasi-arithmetic means
literature_review_status: NOVELTY CLAIM SHOULD TARGET THE SYNTHESIS, NOT COMPONENT THEOREMS
computational_support: Comparative exact and seeded operator audit.
assumptions_scope: Declared candidate observables and summary classes.
allowed_claim: A coarse-graining rule must be specified relative to the observable or dynamics it is meant to preserve.
forbidden_overclaim: A universal proof that no richer common state representation exists.
editorial_action: KEEP AS CENTRAL ORGANIZING PRINCIPLE

short_name: Effective round-trip shortest-path geometry is computational, not yet physical
formal_statement: D_eff is the symmetrized directed shortest-path cost built from ell_ij=exp(q_ij); its metric-like properties are properties of the computational construction.
canonical_version: Legacy geometry track; not fully archived in current repository
closest_prior_literature: Directed shortest-path metrics and symmetrization
computational_support: Legacy tests reported 63-65 packages and 338-350 tests with no software failures.
assumptions_scope: Positive directed edge costs; selected symmetrization.
allowed_claim: The construction defines an effective computational geometry up to global scale.
forbidden_overclaim: Physical distance, spacetime interval, calibrated length, or uniqueness.
editorial_action: ARCHIVE CODE BEFORE USING IN A NEW MANUSCRIPT
next_action: Add the v10.8-v10.9 scripts, summaries, and contracts to the public repository.

short_name: Local simplicial consistency did not establish robust Regge curvature
formal_statement: Selected triangles, tetrahedra, Cayley-Menger determinants, and deficit calculations passed local eligibility checks, but temporal stability and null-separation gates failed across later coarse-graining stages.
canonical_version: Legacy negative geometry track; not fully archived
closest_prior_literature: Regge calculus and discrete geometry diagnostics
computational_support: Reported phase maps, dense ensembles, alternative observables, and persistent regional frames.
assumptions_scope: Specific synthetic q dynamics, topologies, coarse-graining, and gates.
allowed_claim: Robust Regge curvature and promotion to GR remain blocked.
forbidden_overclaim: Emergent gravity, Einstein equations, physical curvature, or successful renormalization.
editorial_action: DO NOT INCLUDE AS POSITIVE THEORY CLAIM
next_action: Archive all v11.x-v12.0 code and define the physical interpretation only after a successful prospective protocol.

short_name: Prospective q-topology coherence signature passed its declared v12.9 gates
formal_statement: On new sizes, topologies, seeds, and data, the declared regional q-topology signature passed 10/10 gates, including joint selectivity in 19/24 cells.
canonical_version: Latest legacy geometry diagnostic; not in current public archive
closest_prior_literature: Graph-signal/topological coherence diagnostics
computational_support: Reported 0/384 gauge mismatches and specified null pass counts.
assumptions_scope: Exact v12.9 generators, masks, thresholds, and factor grid.
allowed_claim: The specific q-topology coherence signature generalized prospectively within the tested ensemble.
forbidden_overclaim: Physical topology, spacetime emergence, curvature, or experimental validation.
editorial_action: ARCHIVE AND CLASSIFY BEFORE CITATION
next_action: Publish the v12.9 source, frozen protocol, and raw cell-level outputs.

short_name: Current minimal architecture is (R,q,mu,K,pi)
formal_statement: The accumulated obstructions motivate a provisional architecture containing relation/order R, relative quantitative marks q, additive measure mu, transition law K, and occupancy pi.
mathematical_status: CONCEPTUAL ARCHITECTURE; NOT A UNIQUENESS THEOREM
closest_prior_literature: Marked relational structures, measure, Markov kernels, and state distributions
computational_support: Each component is motivated by a separate identifiability or closure obstruction.
assumptions_scope: Minimal relative to the obstructions currently studied, not minimal over all possible theories.
allowed_claim: The framework gives a disciplined provisional inventory of information needed for order, measure, transition, and coarse-grained flow.
forbidden_overclaim: A proven unique ontology, a final fundamental theory, or derivation of all primitives.
editorial_action: MAKE THIS THE POSITIVE THESIS OF THE FOUNDATIONAL PAPER
next_action: State explicit independence and reduction questions among q, mu, K, and pi.

short_name: The obstruction map is a framework-level contribution
formal_statement: The project organizes failures of identifiability, scale calibration, law selection, refinement semantics, dynamic closure, noise universality, and macro aggregation into a dependency map.
mathematical_status: SUPPORTED BY MIXED CLASSICAL AND PROJECT-SPECIFIC RESULTS
novelty_status: ORIGINAL ORGANIZATION; INDIVIDUAL ITEMS HAVE UNEQUAL NOVELTY
closest_prior_literature: Pregeometry, causal-set reconstruction, measurement theory, stochastic coarse-graining
computational_support: Canonical audits, failures, corrections, and theorem notes.
assumptions_scope: Only the explicitly studied constructions and axioms.
allowed_claim: The catalogue clarifies which additional structures are logically needed in the current programme.
forbidden_overclaim: Presenting every no-go as equally deep or technically novel.
editorial_action: KEEP, BUT TYPOGRAPHICALLY SEPARATE DEFINITIONS, CLASSICAL COROLLARIES, AND NONTRIVIAL RESULTS

short_name: No physical spacetime, calibrated metric, gravity, or experimental mapping has been derived
formal_statement: The current results establish formal structures and conditional computational properties but do not identify q, mu, K, or pi with measured physical quantities.
closest_prior_literature: Standards of operationalization and theory confirmation
computational_support: Repository records successful, failed, and corrective protocols.
assumptions_scope: Current published artifacts only.
allowed_claim: The work is a formal pre-categorical research programme with explicit open physical bridges.
forbidden_overclaim: Confirmed fundamental physics, emergent GR, matter, energy, or experimental support.
editorial_action: KEEP AS A SHORT BOUNDARY SECTION, NOT A DEFENSIVE REFRAIN
